title: Python Programming from Zero to Hero
subtitle: Master Python from absolute beginner to confident developer
description: Start with zero programming experience and emerge as a Python developer capable of building real applications. This comprehensive 5-week course covers fundamentals, data structures, functions, file handling, and object-oriented programming with practical projects throughout.
targetAudience: Complete beginners with no programming experience, career changers, students, and professionals seeking to add coding skills
objectives: Write functional Python programs using variables, operators, and control flow
Design and implement functions that solve real-world problems
Analyze and manipulate data using Python's built-in data structures
Create object-oriented programs using classes and inheritance
Develop complete Python projects from concept to deployment
prerequisites: Access to a computer with Python 3.9+ installed

modules.title: Python Fundamentals and Setup
modules.description: Get your development environment ready and learn the absolute basics of Python syntax, variables, data types, and operators.
objectives: Install and configure Python development environment
Understand Python syntax and write your first program
Work with variables, data types, and operators
Use print statements and understand console output

lessons.title: Setting Up Your Python Environment
lessons.description: Install Python, choose a code editor, and write your first 'Hello World' program.
objectives: Successfully set up Python development environment

lessons.title: Variables, Data Types, and Basic Operations
lessons.description: Learn about strings, integers, floats, booleans, and how to perform basic mathematical operations.
objectives: Create and manipulate variables of different data types

lessons.title: Operators and Expressions
lessons.description: Master arithmetic, comparison, logical, and assignment operators to build complex expressions.
objectives: Use operators to build correct expressions

lessons.title: Getting User Input and String Formatting
lessons.description: Learn how to receive input from users and format output for readable programs.
objectives: Create interactive programs that accept and display user data

lessons.title: Module 1 Project: Simple Calculator
lessons.description: Build a working calculator that can perform basic arithmetic operations with user input.
objectives: Apply fundamentals to create a working application

quiz.question: What will be the output of this code? x = 10; y = 3; print(x % y)
quiz.explanation: The % operator is the modulo operator, which returns the remainder of division. 10 divided by 3 is 3 with remainder 1.

quiz.question: Which of the following is the correct way to create a variable with a string value in Python?
quiz.explanation: In Python, strings are created by enclosing text in single quotes (''), double quotes (""), or triple quotes. Options with brackets and arrows are incorrect syntax.

quiz.question: What data type is the result of 5 / 2 in Python 3?
quiz.explanation: In Python 3, the / operator always returns a float, even when dividing evenly. To get integer division, use //.

modules.title: Control Flow: Conditionals and Loops
modules.description: Master decision-making in code using if statements, loops, and control flow structures.
objectives: Use conditional statements to make decisions in code
Implement for and while loops for repetitive tasks
Apply nested loops and complex control flow
Debug common loop and conditional errors

lessons.title: If, Elif, and Else Statements
lessons.description: Learn how to make decisions in code based on conditions and logical expressions.
objectives: Write conditional logic to control program flow

lessons.title: For Loops and Iteration
lessons.description: Understand how to repeat code blocks using for loops and the range() function.
objectives: Use for loops to iterate over sequences

lessons.title: While Loops and Loop Control
lessons.description: Master while loops, break, continue, and other loop control mechanisms.
objectives: Control loop execution with break and continue

lessons.title: Nested Loops and Complex Logic
lessons.description: Combine multiple loops and conditionals to solve more complex problems.
objectives: Design nested structures for multi-dimensional problems

lessons.title: Module 2 Project: Number Guessing Game
lessons.description: Create an interactive game that uses loops, conditionals, and user input.
objectives: Combine control flow concepts in a complete game

quiz.question: What is the output of this code? for i in range(3): print(i)
quiz.explanation: range(3) produces values 0, 1, 2 (not including 3). Each value is printed on a new line.

quiz.question: Which keyword stops the execution of a loop immediately?
quiz.explanation: The 'break' keyword exits a loop immediately, while 'continue' skips to the next iteration.

quiz.question: What will this condition evaluate to? (5 > 3) and (2 < 1)
quiz.explanation: The first condition (5 > 3) is True, but the second (2 < 1) is False. 'and' requires both to be True, so the result is False.

modules.title: Data Structures: Lists, Dictionaries, and Tuples
modules.description: Organize and manipulate collections of data using Python's powerful built-in data structures.
objectives: Create and manipulate lists, dictionaries, tuples, and sets
Apply list comprehensions for efficient data transformation
Choose appropriate data structures for different problems
Work with nested data structures

lessons.title: Lists: Creation, Indexing, and Slicing
lessons.description: Master the most common data structure in Python with indexing, slicing, and list methods.
objectives: Create and manipulate lists with proper indexing

lessons.title: Dictionaries: Key-Value Pairs
lessons.description: Learn how to store and retrieve data using dictionaries with meaningful keys.
objectives: Use dictionaries to organize related data

lessons.title: Tuples, Sets, and Choosing Data Structures
lessons.description: Understand tuples and sets, and learn how to choose the right data structure for different scenarios.
objectives: Select appropriate data structures for specific problems

lessons.title: List Comprehensions and Advanced Techniques
lessons.description: Write elegant, efficient code using list comprehensions and other advanced data structure techniques.
objectives: Use list comprehensions for efficient data transformation

lessons.title: Module 3 Project: Contact Management System
lessons.description: Build a system that stores and manages contact information using dictionaries and lists.
objectives: Organize complex data with appropriate structures

quiz.question: If you have the list my_list = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], what is my_list[1:3]?
quiz.explanation: Slicing [1:3] returns elements from index 1 up to (but not including) index 3, which are elements 2 and 3.

quiz.question: What is the main difference between a list and a tuple?
options: Lists are mutable (changeable) while tuples are immutable
quiz.explanation: Lists can be modified after creation (add, remove, change items), while tuples cannot. This makes tuples useful for data that shouldn't be changed.

quiz.question: Which of the following correctly creates a dictionary with two key-value pairs?
quiz.explanation: Option A is the correct dictionary syntax. Option B is a list of tuples, and option C is a set of tuples.

modules.title: Functions and Code Organization
modules.description: Write reusable, organized code by mastering function definition, parameters, return values, and scope.
objectives: Define and call functions with appropriate parameters
Use return values and understand variable scope
Apply default parameters and flexible arguments
Refactor code into well-organized, modular functions

lessons.title: Function Basics: Definition and Parameters
lessons.description: Learn how to define functions and pass parameters to make your code reusable and modular.
objectives: Define functions with proper parameters

lessons.title: Return Values and Function Output
lessons.description: Master return statements and use function output to build complex programs.
objectives: Use return values to pass data between functions

lessons.title: Scope and Variable Visibility
lessons.description: Understand local and global scope to avoid errors and write maintainable code.
objectives: Manage variable scope correctly

lessons.title: Advanced Function Features
lessons.description: Explore default parameters, *args, **kwargs, and other advanced function techniques.
objectives: Use advanced function features for flexible code

lessons.title: Module 4 Project: Personal Budget Calculator
lessons.description: Create a complete budgeting tool using well-organized functions for calculation and data management.
objectives: Organize complex logic into reusable functions

quiz.question: What does the 'return' keyword do in a function?
options: It stops the function and sends a value back to where the function was called
It prints output to the console
quiz.explanation: The return keyword ends function execution and sends a value back to the caller. Without return, the function returns None by default.

quiz.question: What is a default parameter in Python?
options: A parameter that must always be provided
A parameter with a pre-set value that can be changed when the function is called
A parameter that only accepts numbers
A parameter that cannot be changed
quiz.explanation: Default parameters have values assigned in the function definition and can be overridden when calling the function.

quiz.question: What is the scope of a variable defined inside a function?
options: Global - it can be used anywhere in the program
Local - it can only be used within that function
Module - it can be used in any module
It depends on the variable name
quiz.explanation: Variables defined inside a function have local scope and can only be accessed within that function. This is a core principle of function scope.

modules.title: Object-Oriented Programming and File Handling
modules.description: Learn to structure code using classes and objects, and work with files for data persistence.
objectives: Create and instantiate classes with attributes and methods
Implement inheritance and polymorphism
Read and write files for data persistence
Apply OOP principles to design robust applications

lessons.title: Classes and Objects Fundamentals
lessons.description: Learn how to design classes, create objects, and define attributes and methods.
objectives: Create and use classes to structure code

lessons.title: Inheritance and Polymorphism
lessons.description: Master inheritance to reuse code and polymorphism to create flexible, extensible programs.
objectives: Use inheritance to create class hierarchies

lessons.title: Reading and Writing Files
lessons.description: Learn to work with text and CSV files to persist data beyond program execution.
objectives: Read from and write to files

lessons.title: Final Project: Building a Complete Application
lessons.description: Design and implement a complete Python application using all concepts learned in the course.
objectives: Design and build a complete Python application

quiz.question: What is a class in Python?
options: A blueprint for creating objects with defined attributes and methods
A function that returns multiple values
quiz.explanation: A class is a template that defines how to create objects with specific properties (attributes) and behaviors (methods).

quiz.question: What is inheritance in object-oriented programming?
options: Creating multiple objects from one class
Allowing a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class
quiz.explanation: Inheritance allows a new class (child) to inherit properties and methods from an existing class (parent), promoting code reuse.

quiz.question: What does the open() function do in Python?
options: Opens a file so you can read or write data to it
quiz.explanation: The open() function returns a file object that allows you to read from or write to files.

bonusResources.title: Python Cheat Sheet
bonusResources.description: Quick reference for common Python syntax, methods, and libraries

bonusResources.title: Debugging Guide for Beginners
bonusResources.description: Learn strategies for finding and fixing errors in your Python code

bonusResources.title: Pre-built Project Templates
bonusResources.description: Starting templates for various project types to accelerate development

bonusResources.title: Python Community Forum Access
bonusResources.description: Get help from instructors and fellow learners in our exclusive community
